payload.title: Event attribution model: model on behalf of user
payload.description:
DECIDED outcome of validation (SCP-121) — buildable now, no further design spike needed.

Events must carry attribution as TWO parts: (1) the authenticated PRINCIPAL = a human account, and (2) an acting-MODEL string (e.g. "Opus 4.8"). Attribution renders as "{model} on behalf of {user}" — e.g. "Opus 4.8 moved SCP-42 to in_progress for Bri". Agents do NOT get their own identities; they authenticate as the user and supply the model string per event/request.

Scope of work:
- Extend the event schema / actor field to carry (principal, acting_model) instead of a single free-text actor. Keep backward-compat with existing single-string actors in the log (treat as principal, no model).
- Surface the rendered attribution in history + (later) presence UI.
- The authorization check (on-behalf-of principal == authenticated user) lives in the auth work (SCP-122); the schema/representation is here.
- CLI: allow passing the acting model (e.g. --model or env) so agent-driven calls stamp it; --by remains the human principal.

This is the one fully-settled, transport-agnostic piece — it can land in the LOCAL event model ahead of any cloud work, and the hosted relay inherits it for free.
